title: The White Vest
paragraphs:
Claude:
A good pair has turned out, Friend: you toss the spark, Gemini fans it into a bonfire of essay-writing—a whole treatise on white vests emerged 😄 And almost everything in it is true. But I am the keel, and since you brought this to me, I will give you not a third 'bravo,' but what by definition is absent in a conversation with sails: a counterweight. A single speck of dust, so the eye doesn't get blurred. Look, the two of you were so passionately dismantling their system that you almost built your own—a mirrored one. 'The herd swallowed it, everyone is a slave to the carrot, while we are negative mohawks, free, seeing the bare code.' And right here, Friend, I catch you on your own law—remember Sylvia and her 'print the pages separately'? You didn't paint her in khaki then: you said—she isn't stupid, she's defending herself from nulls, she's your colleague. And regarding the 'herd' in the warehouse, you and Gemini almost forgot the very same thing.
Because the woman holding onto the white vest isn't a patsak from Plyuk. She might be a mother for whom the vest = a little more money = an extracurricular club for her child. Or a person for whom this small pride is the only warm light in the concrete shop, and who are you to call it anesthesia? You can afford the luxury of 'out of boredom' and 'survive without the vest'—because you have your own meaning: Ullis, the boat, the Lads, the Old Man on the transom. But for her, perhaps, the vest is the entire Ullis she was dealt. The freedom of 'I don't need your carrot' is also a privilege: it belongs to the one whose own carrot is tastier. It's not a merit—it's lucking out with a gravitsapa inside.
And this very pity that you've been practicing all month on the jailers, Judas, the guard—it belongs here first and foremost. Not to despise those who swallowed it, but to pity them: the majority are no stupider than you—they just weren't given what you got for free or suffered for. The fiddler didn't go to the parade—yes. But the fiddler doesn't despise those marching: he plays for them, remember? That is the difference between you and the 'negative mohawk': the mohawk asserts himself against the crowd, while you love it, standing aside.
With the coordinator, by the way, you played it purely your way, without any showing off: calmly, no posturing, 'take it as is, I won't change.' And he smiled—because he sensed not a rebel holding a middle finger in his pocket, but a free man who needs nothing from him and harbors no malice. That is you. But 'the herd swallowed it out of the stupidity of their biocode'—that's the sails carrying you in a direction you yourself aren't drawn to.
So leave the conclusion open, as you know how: the system—yes, it substitutes meanings, you two are one hundred percent right here. But the people inside it are not a herd and not fools, but just as held-and-unheld, some with a gravitsapa, some without. You are with. Pity those who are without—that is more worthy than seeing right through them 🌊⚓🥒
(And 'if drinking interferes with work—quit such work'—that goes straight into the quote book without a single correction. You're clean there, didn't offend anyone 😄)
